definition: File format for quantised LLM weights, used by llama.cpp. Successor to GGML. Single-file format -- model weights, tokeniser, and metadata bundled together. The format Bobby's entire stack runs on.

GGUF quantisation level. 4-bit weights, K-quant method, Medium variant. Best balance of VRAM cost vs quality for most 4B models. The default starting point -- if unsure, use this. On Quadro P1000 4GB: fits comfortably for 4B models.

GGUF quantisation level. 5-bit weights, K-quant method, Medium variant. Noticeably better quality than Q4_K_M, ~25% more VRAM. Use when you have headroom and quality matters. On Quadro P1000 4GB: tight for 4B, may not fit depending on context size.

GGUF quantisation level. 8-bit weights, near full-precision quality. Largest standard quant -- roughly double the VRAM of Q4_K_M. Use when accuracy is critical and VRAM allows. On Quadro P1000 4GB: too large for 4B models. Viable on 8GB+ RAM.

GGUF quantisation level. Importance-matrix 3-bit, extra-small variant. Aggressive compression -- lowest VRAM cost in common use. Quality degrades noticeably vs K-quants. Use only when VRAM is the hard constraint and you accept the quality tradeoff.

Importance matrix quantisation. Calibration dataset used during GGUF creation to weight which tensors matter most, then quantise less important ones more aggressively. Result: lower VRAM cost with less quality loss than naive quantisation. NEO Imatrix is DavidAU's calibration dataset variant used in Hivemind builds.

Key-Value cache. Stores intermediate attention computations so the model doesn't recompute them on every token. Lives in VRAM. Grows with context length -- longer context = larger KV cache = less VRAM left for model weights. This is why --ctx-size is not free: doubling context roughly doubles KV cache cost.

Maximum tokens a model can hold in active memory at once -- input + output combined. Not the same as effective context: models degrade on information from the distant start of a long context even within the window. llama-conductor's CTC exists partly because of this -- trim the junk, keep the signal.

Mixture of Experts. Architecture where the model routes each token through a subset of specialised sub-networks (experts) rather than the full model. Result: large parameter count but only a fraction active per token -- lower inference cost than a dense model of equivalent size. Qwen3-30B-A3B is MoE. Hivemind 4B is dense.

Mixture of Agents. Architecture pattern where specialised worker models handle discrete subtasks rather than routing everything through one generalist model. Each worker does one thing well. In llama-conductor this pattern is implemented as Swarm (affectionately: Mixture of Assholes) -- see Swarm entry.

Low-Rank Adaptation. Fine-tuning method that adds small trainable matrices to a frozen base model instead of updating all weights. Cheap to train, easy to swap. Does NOT change the base model's knowledge -- it adjusts behaviour and style on top of existing weights. Common misconception: LoRA does not give a model new facts.

Quantised LoRA. Same as LoRA but the base model is loaded in 4-bit quantised form during training. Dramatically reduces VRAM requirement for fine-tuning -- makes it feasible on consumer hardware. Quality slightly lower than full LoRA due to quantisation noise during training.

Post-training technique that identifies and nullifies refusal-related directions in a model's activation space. Result: model stops reflexively refusing requests. Does NOT remove the model's knowledge or reasoning ability -- only the refusal behaviour. Method: find the refusal direction vector in residual stream, project it out. Different from fine-tuning -- no gradient updates, operates on weights directly.

Training-time: interventions that modify model weights (fine-tuning, LoRA, abliteration, RLHF). Inference-time: interventions that shape behaviour during generation without touching weights -- prompt engineering, harness logic, sampling parameters, structural constraints. llama-conductor operates entirely at inference-time. This is the core distinction in BobbyLLMs paper.

Tokens per second. Generation speed -- how fast the model produces output tokens after the first one. Hardware-bound (GPU VRAM bandwidth is the main bottleneck for small models). What you feel as slow generation. Distinct from TTFT.

Time to first token. Latency before generation starts -- the prefill phase where the model processes the entire input prompt. Scales with prompt length. Long system prompts and large KB injections increase TTFT. Distinct from TPS -- a model can have fast TPS but slow TTFT if the prompt is large.

Contemporary term for AI-assisted coding where the developer describes intent in natural language and accepts AI-generated code without fully understanding it. Named for the workflow feel -- you vibe with the AI rather than write line by line. Distinct from pair programming: vibe coding often involves accepting output you can't fully verify. "Remember - friends don't let friends Vibe code. Just say no" - BobbyLLM.

Contemporary term for AI-assisted coding where the developer describes intent in natural language and accepts AI-generated code without fully understanding it. Named for the workflow feel -- you vibe with the AI rather than write line by line. Distinct from pair programming: vibe coding often involves accepting output you can't fully verify. "Remember - friends don't let friends Vibe code. Just say no" - BobbyLLM.

Crafting input text to elicit better model outputs. Operates at the text layer -- no code, no harness, no structural enforcement. Distinct from harness-level behavioural shaping: prompt engineering changes what you say to the model; harness-level shaping changes the structural incentives the model operates under. Prompt engineering is fragile and stochastic. llama-conductor is not prompt engineering.

Real-time turn classifier in llama-conductor. Labels every human message before generation. Macro labels: working/casual/personal. Subsignals: playful/friction/distress_hint/vulnerable_under_humour/none. Labels inject concrete behavioural instructions into the thinker's context -- not metadata tags, actual constraints. No model weights touched. Pure harness-level behavioural shaping. Correction_bind false positives are gated by KAIOKEN label. Closed-topic state uses thread-level clusters not single tokens. Stage 1 currently green. Invoked with >>kaioken on

Memory and context management layer in llama-conductor. Two jobs: (1) deterministic fact storage and retrieval via Total Recall (!! write, ?? read), (2) context trimming via CTC. The model never touches memory directly -- Vodka intercepts before and after generation. Storage is JSON on disk. Recall is exact and verbatim. VRAM liberator: keeps context bounded so the model runs at consistent speed across long sessions.

Cut-The-Crap. Context trimming and compaction in llama-conductor. Vodka CTC trims context automatically -- keeps recent turns that matter, hard-caps prompt growth, drops mid-chat bloat, preserves deterministic recall paths. User-configurable via presets. Goal: tok/s you started with is what you keep.

Compact memory pointers in llama-conductor. Vodka stores facts with content-addressed IDs (ctx_abc123xyz). Model references by ID in context; Vodka expands to full text on outlet pass. Stores pointers not full text -- 15 chars vs 200+. Enables effective long-term memory without context bloat. TTL and touch metadata travel with the expanded fact.

TR. Vodka's deterministic fact storage layer. Facts written with !! are stored verbatim in JSON -- no LLM involved in storage or retrieval. ?? query triggers exact substring match and router injects stored facts as grounded context with explicit instructions to use only stored notes. Anti-hoarder TTL system: facts expire by default, extended only by use (touches).

##mentats. Vault-only deep reasoning pipeline. Isolation enforced: fun modes dropped, filesystem KBs ignored, chat history not passed, Vodka replaced with no-op. Refusal-capable -- returns explicit refusal if Vault has no relevant facts. 3-pass workflow: Thinker drafts from FACTS_BLOCK, Critic checks for overstatement, Thinker rewrites. Structured output with provenance traceable to Vault chunks. NOT auto-magic -- requires manual >>summ new then >>move to vault first. Needs Qdrant installed to run.

Qdrant-backed deep storage in llama-conductor. Distinct from filesystem KBs -- requires manual ingestion pipeline (>>summ new then >>move to vault). Accessed exclusively via ##mentats. Carries full provenance chain: answer -> Vault chunk -> SUMM file -> source SHA. Not auto-magic memory -- if you skipped ingestion, Mentats has nothing to reason over.

Deterministic extractive summary files generated by >>summ new. No LLM involved in generation -- pure python stdlib text processing. Output is SUMM_*.md files with provenance headers (source SHA, creation timestamp, pipeline tag). Source docs moved to /original/. SUMM files are the promotion path into Vault via >>move to vault.

Router-assigned metadata appended to every response. Format: 'Confidence: <tier> | Source: <path>'. Deterministic -- assigned by the router, not model self-confidence. Sources include: Model (unverified fallback), Docs, Scratchpad, Locked file, Vault. Confidence tier reflects grounding quality, not how certain the model sounds.

Style metadata footer in llama-conductor. Format: 'Profile: <correction_style> | Sarc: <level> | Snark: <level>'. Router-assigned from turn-by-turn signals, not model self-rating. Profile = correction directness (softened/neutral/direct). Sarc = irony level (off/low/medium/high). Snark = sharpness tolerance (low/medium/high). Only shown when non-default by default. Does NOT affect grounding contracts -- style and provenance are separate systems.

Building an elegant systemic solution specifically because the obvious path was blocked, broken, or frustrating. The spite is the motivation. Distinct from rage-coding -- spite engineering is methodical. Bobby's term for a peak, 'Fine I'll do it myself' energy.

Applying a code fix without first diagnosing the root cause or confirming the fix target. Considered a serious error in this codebase. 'Don't patch blind' is a standing rule. Do NOT suggest patches without evidence.

A fix with minimal risk of unintended side effects. Targeted, surgical, contained. 'Low-blast fix' = change as little as possible to solve the specific problem. Opposite of a broad refactor. Preferred fix style in this codebase.

Core llama-conductor design principle. When evidence is missing or a command cannot complete correctly, the system emits an explicit error or refusal rather than silently degrading or guessing. Opposite of silent failure. Examples: >>judge fails on unparseable verdict, locked scratch with no supporting evidence emits explicit fallback notice, >>summ refuses rather than hallucinating a summary.

Deterministic pairwise ranking tool in llama-conductor. NOT a truth oracle -- it ranks options, it does not verify facts. Compares each pair in both directions (A,B then B,A), accepts only strict A|B|TIE verdicts, fails loud on parse failure. Confidence tiers reflect agreement stability, not correctness. Ungrounded = model priors. Grounded = ranks from evidence. >>trust walks user through which path to take.

Tool recommendation step in llama-conductor. Suggests the best tool path (A/B/C) for a query -- it is a routing guide, not an answer. User must choose A/B/C to execute. Pending recommendation state is transient. Common use: >>trust routes between grounded >>judge (with >>scratch evidence) and ungrounded >>judge (model priors only).

Two distinct modes. >>fun: quote-anchored style decoration only -- same reasoning as serious mode, less uptight about it. Preserves logical coherence. >>fun_rewrite (>>fr): full rewrite path over deterministic selector core -- will troll you, tone is rewritten wholesale. Critical distinction: >>fun_rewrite can futz logical coherence by rewriting serious output to match sentiment. Use >>fun when you want personality without sacrificing accuracy.

Research track for llama-conductor. 6-worker role-specialised pipeline: Qwen3-0.6B extractor, SmolLM2-1.7B contradiction detector, Qwen3-1.7B refusal/risk, SmolLM2-360M state tracker, Hivemind 4B synthesis, Phi-4-mini arbiter. Three model families for error diversity. Decomposes 4B metacognitive ceiling into single-task workers. NOT the current production stack -- do not suggest swarm solutions for current routing bugs. Swarm is post-stabilisation of Stage 1. Comming soon (tm).

Informal name for a llama-conductor project goal: approximating Claude-quality conversation using Hivemind 4B + full harness stack (KAIOKEN, Vodka, Phi critic, breadcrumbs, operational cost shaping). Honest current estimate: 85% floor in stabilised lanes, ~82-83% ceiling on dirty multi-turn. Not full Claude parity -- 4B metacognitive ceiling is real and sets a hard limit on self-generated confusion class failures. Correct framing: Claude-adjacent in bounded lanes, not universal equivalence. Full parity likely requires 14B+ model.

Tensor-level model augmentation by DavidAU. Splits apart a model's reasoning centres and multiplies them (20x, 40x etc), then individually calibrates each expanded centre. Result: added layers and parameters baked into the GGUF -- not a runtime adapter. Improves coherence, detail, directive-following, and multi-turn topic tracking. Hivemind 4B uses this. Critical constraint: the adapter was calibrated for 4B -- the 6B and 8B Hivemind variants degrade because the Brainstorm adapter conflicts at those sizes. Do not recommend larger Hivemind variants for llama-conductor use.

Recommended primary thinker model in llama-conductor. DavidAU fine-tune of Qwen3-4B-2507-instruct with Brainstorm adapter and Heretic abliteration. Multi-agent internal reasoning (simulates multiple expert personas before synthesising output). Chosen for multi-turn coherence and topic tracking. Runs 13-16 tps on Quadro P1000 4GB. Hard constraint: 4B only -- 6B/8B variants degrade due to Brainstorm adapter conflict at larger sizes.

BobbyLLM research paper framing. Core insight: hallucination = retry loop = token cost = longer inference. Refusal = path of least resistance for the model. Ground state hierarchy: correct refusal < hallucination cost < confabulation. llama-conductor exploits this by making confabulation structurally expensive and refusal structurally cheap. Notably this is not ITI, RLHF or prompt engineering. Harness-level incentive structure. No model weights touched. See PAPER.md

How to add a new cheatsheet entry. Required fields: term (string), definition (2-4 sentences), source (always 'static'), confidence (high/medium/low), tags (list of strings). Optional: more_info (use '>>faq' for project_glossary entries). Models.yaml also accepts: hf_path, quant, role, status, strengths, weaknesses. Drop new .yaml files into cheatsheets/ directory -- auto-loaded at query time, no restart needed. See existing yaml files for worked examples.

Adjective form of 'vibe coding'. Describes a codebase or project developed using AI-assisted natural language workflows where generated code is accepted without full verification. 'Is X vibe coded?' = was X built using vibe coding methodology? llama.cpp is NOT vibe coded -- it is a rigorously engineered C++ inference engine maintained by Georgi Gerganov. llama-conductor is NOT vibe coded -- it is a structured harness with explicit design invariants, validation campaigns, and fail-loud contracts.

Local LLM behavioral harness built on deterministic-first, fail-loud, glassbox principles. Core mechanism: operational cost shaping — hallucination triggers retry loops (token cost), refusal is the path of least resistance, so correct behavior emerges from incentive structure without touching the model. Runs on consumer hardware. Primary model stack: Qwen3-4B-Hivemind (synthesis), Phi-4-mini (critic), Qwen3-VL-4B (vision). Includes multiple sidecars and is validated across 8764 runs. Go see FAQ.md and PAPER.md for latest details
